description: Raw fz_ir Term::Call/TailCall currently require callee: FnId, so provider-boundary calls are represented as synthetic __external__ FnId bodies plus Module.external_call_edges keyed by CallsiteId. Link then rewrites those callsites to provider FnIds, planner re-derives CallEdgeTarget::External from the side table, metadata imports are collected from the side table, and native codegen rejects unresolved side-table edges. This is old-world shape: callable identity, local body identity, and provider-boundary import identity are conflated. Replace direct-call callee identity with a first-class target model that can represent local body targets and provider-boundary symbols directly; derive import metadata from call targets; remove synthetic external stubs and authoritative ExternalCallEdge side table after linker/planner/codegen/interpreter migration.

comments.text: Implemented raw IR provider-boundary target model: Term::Call/TailCall now carry DirectCallTarget::{Local, ProviderBoundary}; import edges are derived from provider-boundary term targets; ir_lower no longer synthesizes __external__ halt stubs; linker rewrites provider-boundary calls to Local(FnId); planner CallEdgeTarget variant is ProviderBoundary rather than External. Verified cargo check --lib, cargo test --lib --no-run, fz_ir::, runtime_unit_metadata_carries_external_import_refs, codegen_rejects_unresolved_external_module_calls, image_linker_rejects_unresolved_external_imports_without_provider, linked_ir_units_rewrite_provider_boundary_calls_and_run_provider_body, planner_keeps_external_module_calls_at_provider_boundary, planner_keeps_provider_protocol_calls_at_external_boundary, runtime_graph_mixed_enum_take_calls_plan_range_specialization, and frontend_only_checked_interp_rejects_unlinked_process_heap_alloc_stats. Wider cargo test --lib compiler2:: still has pre-existing source/macro and enum/type-infer failures tracked by fz-rh2.16.11.5.1 and fz-rh2.17.5.6.6.

title: Modernize raw IR direct-call targets: replace external stubs/side-table edges with first-class provider-boundary call targets
